docs.title: AxiomBraid 2.0
docs.text:
AxiomBraid is an explainable, safety-first toolkit for dataset inspection, validation, conservative cleaning, reporting, comparison, drift screening, controlled corruption, and evaluation.

import axiombraid as AB
result = AB.inspect("students.csv", include_confidence=True, include_quality_profile=True)
AB.report("students.csv", include_confidence=True, include_quality_profile=True)

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Preview before mutation
  • Safe functional defaults
  • Explainable findings
  • Reproducible workflows
  • Version 1-compatible workflows in Version 2

See Version 2 Release Notes and Migration from 1.x.

docs.title: Core
docs.text:
frame = AB.read_csv("data.csv")
inspection = AB.inspect(frame)
AB.report(frame)
cleaned = AB.clean(frame, risk="low")

docs.title: Explainability
docs.text: result = AB.inspect(frame, include_confidence=True, include_quality_profile=True)

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
corrupted, truth = AB.inject_issues(frame, missing_rate=0.05, duplicate_rate=0.05, random_state=42)
results = AB.run_evaluation(frame, corruption_config={"missing_rate": 0.05, "duplicate_rate": 0.05, "random_state": 42})

docs.title: Benchmarks
docs.text:
benchmark = AB.benchmark_inspection(frame, repeats=3)
scaling = AB.benchmark_scaling(frame, sizes=[100, 1000, 5000], repeats=2)

docs.title: API Stability Policy
docs.text:
AxiomBraid follows semantic versioning.

  • Patch releases (2.0.x) provide compatible fixes.
  • Minor releases (2.x.0) may add compatible functionality.
  • Incompatible changes require a new major version.

Names exported through axiombraid.__all__ form the stable Version 2 top-level API. A public API targeted for removal will normally receive a documented deprecation period. Internal and private names may change.

Version 2 preserves supported Version 1 workflows. Use AB.compatibility_check() for a runtime diagnostic.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
AxiomBraid separates responsibilities into focused modules:

  • inspector: profiling, issues, quality scores, reports
  • cleaning: preview-first actions, audit logs, undo
  • validation: reusable data contracts
  • comparison: before/after, schema, and drift screens
  • governance: fingerprints and leakage checks
  • streaming: memory-bounded CSV profiling
  • batch: folder analysis and safe parallel execution
  • cache: content- and configuration-aware result caching
  • plugins: read-only extension hooks
  • api: stable pandas-style functional surface

The main safety boundary is that automated outlier deletion, invalid-range clipping, constant-column dropping, and identifier removal are not performed silently.

docs.title: Benchmarking
docs.text:
Run the local benchmark:

py benchmarks\benchmark_release.py

Results depend on hardware, Python, pandas, operating system, and dataset shape. Baselines are evidence for regression detection, not universal performance claims.

The benchmark records:

  • full inspection duration
  • cached inspection miss/hit duration
  • streaming CSV duration
  • row and column counts
  • environment metadata

docs.title: Command-Line Interface
docs.text:
py -m axiombraid --version
py -m axiombraid inspect data.csv --confidence --quality-profile
py -m axiombraid inspect data.csv --format console --format json --format html --output reports/data
py -m axiombraid evaluate clean.csv --missing-rate 0.05 --duplicate-rate 0.05 --random-state 42 --output reports/evaluation
py -m axiombraid benchmark data.csv --repeats 3 --output reports/benchmark.json
py -m axiombraid benchmark data.csv --sizes 100,1000,5000 --repeats 2 --output reports/scaling.json
py -m axiombraid batch datasets --output reports --workers 4 --format json --format html
py -m axiombraid stream large.csv --chunksize 100000 --sample-rows 50000
py -m axiombraid cache-inspect data.csv
py -m axiombraid validate data.csv contract.json
py -m axiombraid fingerprint data.csv
py -m axiombraid init-config axiombraid.yaml
py -m axiombraid themes

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
AxiomBraid accepts JSON, YAML, and TOML configuration files.

analysis:
  high_cardinality_threshold: 0.90
  low_missing_threshold: 5
  high_missing_threshold: 30
performance:
  mode: auto
  sample_rows: 50000
  strategy: random
  random_state: 42
report:
  language: en
  html_theme: light

import axiombraid as AB
guide = AB.Guide.from_config("data.csv", "axiombraid.yaml")

docs.title: Doxygen Source Documentation
docs.text: AxiomBraid 2.0.0 includes a final Doxygen configuration for API and source-code documentation.

docs.title: Included files
docs.text:
Doxyfile
 generate_doxygen.cmd
 generate_doxygen.ps1
 .github/workflows/doxygen.yml

The recommended Windows command is:

.\generate_doxygen.cmd

The CMD script avoids PowerShell execution-policy and digital-signature restrictions.

docs.title: Install Doxygen and Graphviz on Windows
docs.text:
winget install --id DimitriVanHeesch.Doxygen -e
winget install --id Graphviz.Graphviz -e

Close and reopen the terminal, then verify:

doxygen --version
dot -V

When the tools are installed but not yet visible in the current PowerShell session:

$env:Path += ";C:\Program Files\doxygen\bin"
$env:Path += ";C:\Program Files\Graphviz\bin"

docs.title: Generate the documentation
docs.text:
From the project root:

.\generate_doxygen.cmd

Direct Doxygen command:

doxygen .\Doxyfile

PowerShell script alternative:

Unblock-File .\generate_doxygen.ps1
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Force
.\generate_doxygen.ps1

docs.title: Generated files
docs.text:
Main page:

docs/doxygen/html/index.html

Warnings log:

docs/doxygen/doxygen-warnings.log

Doxygen tag file:

docs/doxygen/axiombraid-2.0.0.tag

Open the documentation manually:

Start-Process .\docs\doxygen\html\index.html

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text:
• Python packages and modules
  • Public classes, functions, and methods
  • Python docstrings and signatures
  • Source-code browser and cross-references
  • README and Markdown documentation
  • Example scripts
  • Class, dependency, and directory diagrams through Graphviz

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text: The Doxygen Documentation workflow generates the HTML documentation and uploads it as a downloadable workflow artifact. It does not replace the existing MkDocs website workflow.

docs.title: Sharing with a reviewer
docs.text:
Send:

Doxyfile

and optionally ZIP the generated HTML folder:

Compress-Archive -Path .\docs\doxygen\html\* -DestinationPath .\AxiomBraid-2.0.0-Doxygen-HTML.zip -Force

docs.title: Stable PyPI installation
docs.text:
pip install axiombraid

Windows:

py -m pip install axiombraid

Upgrade:

py -m pip install --upgrade axiombraid

Optional charts:

pip install "axiombraid[charts]"

Development:

git clone https://github.com/Adil307/AxiomBraid.git
cd AxiomBraid
py -m pip install -e ".[dev]"

Local wheel:

py -m pip install dist/axiombraid-2.0.0-py3-none-any.whl

Verify:

py -c "import axiombraid as AB; print(AB.__version__, AB.API_STATUS)"
py -m axiombraid --version

Expected:

2.0.0 stable
AxiomBraid 2.0.0

Python 3.10 through 3.14 are supported.

docs.title: Migration from AxiomBraid 1.x to 2.0
docs.text:
Most users only need to upgrade:

py -m pip install --upgrade axiombraid

Existing code remains valid:

import axiombraid as AB
frame = AB.read_csv("data.csv")
result = AB.inspect(frame)
AB.report(frame)
cleaned = AB.clean(frame, risk="low")

New confidence and quality output is opt-in:

result = AB.inspect(frame, include_confidence=True, include_quality_profile=True)

Metadata changes:

API_STATUS: stable
PUBLIC_API_VERSION: 2
VERSION_INFO: (2, 0, 0)

Confidence is evidence strength, not probability. Evaluation metrics use issue/column granularity. Validity covers supported rules rather than every possible business rule.

docs.title: Migration from DataGuidePy
docs.text:
Old code continues to work in 0.9 with a DeprecationWarning:

import dataguidepy

Recommended replacement:

import axiombraid as AB

The old namespace and dataguide command are scheduled for removal in 1.0.

docs.title: Stable Public API — Version 2
docs.text: import axiombraid as AB

docs.title: Version 1-compatible core
docs.text:
• AB.read_csv, AB.read_excel, AB.guide
  • AB.inspect, AB.report, AB.clean, AB.validate
  • AB.compare, AB.detect_drift, AB.export_html
  • AB.stream_csv, AB.cached_inspect, AB.batch_analyze
  • AB.Guide, AB.DataGuide, AB.BatchAnalyzer, AB.InspectionCache

docs.title: Version 2
docs.text:
• AB.inspect_with_confidence, AB.issue_confidence, AB.add_confidence, AB.confidence_report
  • AB.quality_profile, AB.build_quality_profile, AB.format_quality_profile_console
  • AB.inject_issues, AB.ground_truth_pairs
  • AB.evaluate_detection, AB.evaluate_quality_response, AB.run_evaluation, AB.evaluation_report
  • AB.benchmark_inspection, AB.benchmark_scaling, AB.suggest_confidence_thresholds
  • AB.format_evaluation_console, AB.format_benchmark_console, AB.compatibility_check

docs.title: Diagnostics and metadata
docs.text:
• AB.about, AB.self_check
  • AB.__version__, AB.VERSION_INFO, AB.API_STATUS, AB.PUBLIC_API_VERSION, AB.RELEASE_STAGE, AB.BRAND_NAME

Names exported through axiombraid.__all__ are supported. Confidence is evidence strength, not probability. Evaluation metrics use issue/column granularity.

docs.title: AxiomBraid Quickstart
docs.text:
import axiombraid as AB

result = AB.inspect("students.csv")
AB.report("students.csv")
cleaned = AB.clean("students.csv")

AB is the official documentation alias. Python aliases are user-defined, but all AxiomBraid examples consistently use uppercase AB as requested.

docs.title: AxiomBraid 2.0 Release Notes
docs.text: AxiomBraid 2.0 adds evidence-aware confidence, a five-dimensional quality profile, controlled corruption, exact ground truth, detection evaluation, quality-response analysis, and benchmarks while preserving supported Version 1 workflows.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Human-readable confidence reports
  • Completeness, Uniqueness, Validity, Consistency, and Integrity scores
  • Synthetic missingness, duplicates, text issues, range errors, outliers, date formats, constants, and identifiers
  • Precision, recall, F1, per-detector metrics, and baseline subtraction
  • Runtime, memory, and scaling benchmarks
  • Expanded diagnostics, migration guidance, and API stability policy
pip install axiombraid

docs.title: Research readiness
docs.text:
Version 1.0 provides a stable software base for future empirical evaluation, but a research paper still requires benchmark datasets, ground-truth issue labels, comparisons with existing tools, reproducible experiment scripts, statistical analysis, and clearly scoped novelty claims.

Recommended study questions include:

  1. How accurately does normalized text inconsistency detection identify real issues?
  2. Does risk-classified cleaning reduce harmful automatic transformations?
  3. How often does identifier screening reduce leakage-prone features?
  4. Does beginner-oriented explanation improve task accuracy or completion time?

Report precision, recall, F1, false-positive rate, runtime, memory, and harmful-change counts. Human studies should follow university ethics and informed-consent procedures.

docs.title: Security and Privacy Review
docs.text:
• Dataset fingerprints are SHA-256 digests and do not intentionally include raw values.
  • Cache entries contain inspection results and may include example values from detected issues; store caches in an access-controlled location when data is sensitive.
  • Plugins receive deep copies, but plugin code is trusted local Python code and can access the host environment.
  • HTML reports escape dataset-derived text before rendering.
  • Cleaning never changes caller data through the functional API and sampled cleaning is blocked.
  • AxiomBraid does not upload datasets or make network requests.

docs.title: AxiomBraid 2.0 — Phase 1 Development Alpha
docs.text: Development version: 2.0.0a1 Status: Alpha — local/development testing only Phase 1 feature: Explainable issue-confidence layer

docs.title: What Phase 1 Adds
docs.text:
Version 1 features remain available. Phase 1 adds optional confidence metadata to detected issues.

The confidence value is not a statistical probability. It is a transparent, deterministic evidence-strength score.

docs.title: Backward-Compatible Usage
docs.text:
Existing code still works:

import axiombraid as AB

df = AB.read_csv("data.csv")
result = AB.inspect(df)
AB.report(df)
cleaned = AB.clean(df, risk="low")

By default, AB.inspect(df) keeps the previous result shape and does not add confidence fields.

docs.title: New Phase 1 Usage
docs.text:
result = AB.inspect(df, include_confidence=True)

for issue in result["issues"]:
    print(issue["code"])
    print(issue["confidence"]["score"])
    print(issue["confidence"]["level"])
    print(issue["confidence"]["evidence"])

Convenience function:

result = AB.inspect_with_confidence(df)

Add confidence to an already-created inspection result:

result = AB.inspect(df)
enhanced = AB.add_confidence(result)

Score one issue directly:

confidence = AB.issue_confidence(result["issues"][0])
print(confidence)

docs.title: Confidence Meaning
docs.text:
• score: Evidence-strength heuristic from 0.0 to 1.0.
  • level: high, medium, or low.
  • method: deterministic_rule or explainable_heuristic.
  • evidence: Human-readable reason for the score.
  • is_probability: Always False in Phase 1.

docs.title: Why This Is Useful
docs.text:
A user can now distinguish between:

  • Exact findings such as duplicates and missing values.
  • Heuristic findings such as possible outliers and identifier-like columns.

This keeps AxiomBraid explainable instead of pretending that every detection has equal certainty.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: Phase 1 does not change the Version 1 cleaning behavior. Confidence metadata is advisory and does not automatically delete, replace, or modify data.

docs.title: AxiomBraid 2.0 Phase 2 — Evidence-Aware Confidence
docs.text:
Development version: 2.0.0a2 Status: Alpha development build Public release status: Not intended as the final 2.0.0 release

Phase 2 extends the optional confidence layer introduced in Phase 1. The core Version 1 workflow remains available, and confidence remains opt-in.

docs.title: What Phase 2 adds
docs.text:
• Detector-specific evidence instead of one generic explanation.
  • Per-column confidence for column-based findings.
  • Configurable confidence-level thresholds.
  • Configurable detector base scores.
  • Rich confidence factors for auditability.
  • Aggregate confidence statistics.
  • A compact AB.confidence_report() helper.

docs.title: Backward-compatible default
docs.text:
result = AB.inspect(df)

The default inspection result is unchanged by the confidence layer.

docs.title: Enable confidence
docs.text:
result = AB.inspect(
    df,
    include_confidence=True,
)

Or:

result = AB.inspect_with_confidence(df)

docs.title: Inspect evidence for one issue
docs.text:
for issue in result["issues"]:
    confidence = issue["confidence"]

    print(issue["code"])
    print(confidence["score"])
    print(confidence["level"])
    print(confidence["method"])
    print(confidence["evidence"])
    print(confidence["factors"])
    print(confidence["per_column"])

docs.title: Per-column example
docs.text:
For a potential-outlier finding, a column-level result can include:

score
level
method
IQR evidence
outlier count
outlier percentage
lower bound
upper bound
IQR multiplier

For date-like text, evidence can include:

parsed count
non-missing count
parse percentage
suggested dtype

For text inconsistencies, evidence can include:

normalized group count
variant count

docs.title: Custom confidence thresholds
docs.text:
custom_confidence = {
    "level_thresholds": {
        "high": 0.95,
        "medium": 0.85,
    }
}

result = AB.inspect(
    df,
    include_confidence=True,
    confidence_config=custom_confidence,
)

The score does not have to change when thresholds change; only the label can change.

docs.title: Customize a detector base score
docs.text:
custom_confidence = {
    "detectors": {
        "potential_outliers": {
            "base_score": 0.72,
        }
    }
}

AxiomBraid merges partial confidence configuration with safe defaults.

docs.title: Compact confidence report
docs.text:
inspection = AB.inspect(df)
report = AB.confidence_report(inspection)

print(report["summary"])
print(report["issues"])

docs.title: Important interpretation rule
docs.text:
Confidence is explicitly returned with:

is_probability: false

The score represents deterministic evidence strength. It is not a calibrated probability that a finding is correct.

docs.title: Research relevance
docs.text:
Phase 2 makes confidence output more suitable for future evaluation because the system now exposes:

  • the detector used,
  • the evidence supporting the score,
  • the factors used,
  • per-column assessments,
  • configurable thresholds,
  • aggregate confidence statistics.

Calibration against controlled ground truth is a later phase and should not be claimed yet.

docs.title: AxiomBraid Version 2 — Phase 3
docs.text:
Development version: 2.0.0a3

Phase 3 makes the confidence system understandable to normal users while preserving the machine-readable structures added in Phases 1 and 2.

docs.title: Main improvements
docs.text:
• Human-friendly terminal confidence reports.
  • AB.report(..., include_confidence=True).
  • Summary-only or full confidence detail levels.
  • User-friendly issue names instead of raw detector codes.
  • Simple evidence explanations.
  • Confidence-aware recommended actions.
  • Confidence sections in HTML reports.
  • Confidence metadata in JSON exports.
  • Collapsible technical details in HTML.
  • Roman Urdu confidence explanations.
  • CLI --confidence support for console, JSON, and HTML inspection reports.

docs.title: Recommended user workflow
docs.text:
import axiombraid as AB

# Load data
df = AB.read_csv("Messy_Employee_dataset.csv")

# Human-readable terminal report with confidence
AB.report(
    df,
    include_confidence=True,
)

The terminal now shows readable sections such as:

CONFIDENCE OVERVIEW
- Issues assessed: 6
- High confidence: 4
- Medium confidence: 2
- Low confidence: 0
- Average evidence strength: 90%

CONFIDENCE DETAILS
1. Potential Outliers
   Severity: HIGH
   Confidence: 82% (MEDIUM)
   Column(s): Age
   Evidence: 1 potential outlier was detected in 'Age'...
   Recommended action: Review the flagged values before changing or removing them...

The terminal output intentionally does not print nested per_column dictionaries or internal detector method names.

docs.title: Summary-only console mode
docs.text:
AB.report(
    df,
    include_confidence=True,
    confidence_details="summary",
)

Available values:

summary
full

docs.title: Confidence-only readable report
docs.text:
The Phase 2 compact dictionary behavior remains unchanged:

inspection = AB.inspect(df)
compact = AB.confidence_report(inspection)

To print a readable confidence-only report:

AB.confidence_report(
    inspection,
    display=True,
)

docs.title: HTML report with confidence
docs.text:
AB.export_html(
    df,
    "employee_report.html",
    include_confidence=True,
    theme="dark",
)

The HTML report contains:

  • Confidence overview cards.
  • High/medium/low confidence counts.
  • Average evidence strength.
  • One readable card for each issue.
  • Severity and affected columns.
  • Simple evidence.
  • Recommended action.
  • Collapsible technical details.

docs.title: JSON report with confidence
docs.text:
guide = AB.Guide(df)

guide.export_json(
    "employee_report.json",
    include_confidence=True,
)

The JSON contains machine-readable fields including:

confidence_summary
confidence_recommendations
issues[*].confidence

Default JSON export remains backward compatible and does not include confidence unless requested.

docs.title: Roman Urdu confidence report
docs.text:
AB.report(
    df,
    language="roman_urdu",
    include_confidence=True,
)

docs.title: Command line
docs.text:
Readable terminal report:

py -m axiombraid inspect data.csv --confidence

Summary-only confidence:

py -m axiombraid inspect data.csv --confidence --confidence-details summary

Console, JSON, and HTML together:

py -m axiombraid inspect data.csv `
  --confidence `
  --format console `
  --format json `
  --format html `
  --output reports\data

docs.title: Interpretation rule
docs.text:
A confidence score represents evidence strength, not a calibrated probability.

For example:

Confidence: 82%

means the detector has an evidence-strength score of 0.82. It does not mean there is an 82% statistical probability that the finding is correct.

docs.title: Explainable Multi-Dimensional Data Quality Profile
docs.text:
Phase 4 adds a new, opt-in quality profile that explains why a dataset score is high or low.

The existing data_quality score is preserved for backward compatibility.

docs.title: Quick usage
docs.text:
import axiombraid as AB

profile = AB.quality_profile("data.csv")
print(profile["score"])
print(profile["dimensions"])

Or include it in a normal inspection:

result = AB.inspect(
    "data.csv",
    include_quality_profile=True,
)

print(result["quality_profile"])

Human-readable console report:

AB.report(
    "data.csv",
    include_quality_profile=True,
)

HTML report:

AB.export_html(
    "data.csv",
    "report.html",
    include_quality_profile=True,
)

CLI:

py -m axiombraid inspect data.csv --quality-profile

docs.title: Completeness
docs.text: Measures missing cells.

docs.title: Uniqueness
docs.text: Measures exact duplicate rows.

docs.title: Validity
docs.text:
Measures violations detected by supported conservative numeric-range rules.

A score of 100 does not prove universal domain validity.

docs.title: Consistency
docs.text: Measures supported text-representation inconsistencies and lightly weights strongly date-like text stored as generic text.

docs.title: Integrity
docs.text:
Currently measures observable structural usefulness through constant-column detection.

It does not claim relational or referential integrity without explicit external keys or contracts.

docs.title: Default weights
docs.text:
Completeness  30%
Uniqueness    20%
Validity      20%
Consistency   20%
Integrity     10%

Weights can be customized:

profile = AB.quality_profile(
    df,
    quality_config={
        "weights": {
            "completeness": 4,
            "uniqueness": 1,
            "validity": 1,
            "consistency": 1,
            "integrity": 1,
        }
    },
)

AxiomBraid normalizes the supplied non-negative weights so they sum to 1.0.

docs.title: Important scientific limitation
docs.text: The Version 2 quality profile is a transparent heuristic summary of checks that AxiomBraid can observe. It is not a universal scientific standard and does not prove semantic or domain correctness.

docs.title: Controlled Synthetic Corruption and Ground Truth
docs.text: Phase 5 adds a non-destructive corruption engine for controlled product testing. It is not a research-paper folder and it does not publish private research notes.

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
import axiombraid as AB

corrupted_df, ground_truth = AB.inject_issues(
    clean_df,
    missing_rate=0.05,
    duplicate_rate=0.05,
    text_case_rate=0.05,
    whitespace_rate=0.05,
    invalid_range_rate=0.05,
    outlier_rate=0.02,
    date_format_rate=0.10,
    constant_columns=1,
    identifier_columns=1,
    random_state=42,
)

docs.title: Supported controlled issues
docs.text:
• Missing values
  • Exact duplicate rows
  • Text case inconsistencies
  • Leading/trailing whitespace
  • Invalid conservative numeric ranges
  • Numerical outliers
  • Mixed date formatting
  • Constant columns
  • Identifier-like columns

docs.title: Ground-truth design
docs.text:
Every injected event records:

  • issue code
  • affected columns
  • affected row indices
  • cell locations where applicable
  • original values
  • injected values
  • configuration and random state

AxiomBraid evaluates at issue/column granularity because that is the granularity of public inspection findings.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text:
• The source DataFrame is never mutated.
  • A fixed random_state makes corruption reproducible.
  • The ground truth records only intentionally injected issues.
  • Pre-existing source-data problems remain possible and are handled by Phase 6 baseline subtraction.

docs.title: Evaluation, Tuning, Performance, and Compatibility
docs.text: Phase 6 turns the Phase 5 corruption engine into a complete product-validation workflow.

docs.title: One-command evaluation
docs.text:
import axiombraid as AB

result = AB.evaluation_report(
    clean_df,
    corruption_config={
        "missing_rate": 0.05,
        "duplicate_rate": 0.05,
        "invalid_range_rate": 0.05,
        "outlier_rate": 0.02,
        "constant_columns": 1,
        "random_state": 42,
    },
)

The result includes:

  • corrupted DataFrame
  • exact ground truth
  • clean baseline inspection
  • corrupted inspection
  • overall detection metrics
  • per-detector metrics
  • confidence diagnostics
  • quality-score response

docs.title: Detection metrics
docs.text:
metrics = result["detection_evaluation"]["overall"]

print(metrics["precision"])
print(metrics["recall"])
print(metrics["f1"])

Metrics are calculated at issue/column granularity. Injected issue types already present in the clean baseline are listed as preexisting_expected_pairs and excluded from scoring.

docs.title: Direct evaluation
docs.text:
corrupted_df, truth = AB.inject_issues(clean_df, missing_rate=0.05)

baseline = AB.inspect(clean_df, include_confidence=True)
candidate = AB.inspect(corrupted_df, include_confidence=True)

metrics = AB.evaluate_detection(
    candidate,
    truth,
    baseline_inspection=baseline,
)

docs.title: Quality-score response
docs.text:
response = AB.evaluate_quality_response(clean_df, corrupted_df)

print(response["clean_score"])
print(response["corrupted_score"])
print(response["dimension_deltas"])

docs.title: Runtime and memory benchmark
docs.text:
benchmark = AB.benchmark_inspection(
    clean_df,
    repeats=3,
)

print(AB.format_benchmark_console(benchmark))

docs.title: Scaling benchmark
docs.text:
scaling = AB.benchmark_scaling(
    clean_df,
    sizes=[100, 1000, 5000],
    repeats=2,
)

Peak memory uses Python tracemalloc; native-library allocations may not all be included.

docs.title: Empirical confidence-threshold suggestion
docs.text:
suggestion = AB.suggest_confidence_thresholds(
    result["detection_evaluation"],
    minimum_true_positives=3,
)

This is a transparent tuning aid, not probability calibration. Validate any suggestion on independent datasets before adopting it.

docs.title: Compatibility check
docs.text:
print(AB.compatibility_check())

The compatibility check verifies the stable Version 1 API and the new Version 2 APIs.

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
py -m axiombraid evaluate data.csv --output reports\evaluation
py -m axiombraid benchmark data.csv --repeats 3 --output reports\benchmark.json
py -m axiombraid benchmark data.csv --sizes 100,1000,5000 --repeats 2

docs.title: Version 2 Phase 7 — Final Hardening
docs.text:
Phase 7 converts the alpha line into stable 2.0.0.

Completed:

  • Stable synchronized metadata
  • Centralized runtime version module
  • Production/stable packaging classifier and project URLs
  • Cache isolation from alpha builds
  • Expanded self-check and compatibility checks
  • Final regression tests
  • Migration, stability, release, and publication documentation
  • Wheel, source distribution, Twine, and fresh-install verification

Confidence remains evidence strength rather than probability. Evaluation operates at issue/column granularity. Risky cleaning remains review-first.
